purpose: Function purpose: "The function processes command-line arguments to configure and execute the encoding of images or sequences into the HEIF format using specified parameters and options."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Initializes the libheif library and manages its resources.  
2. Parses command-line options and arguments for various encoding parameters.  
3. Validates input parameters and options for encoding settings.  
4. Loads encoder plugins based on specified options.  
5. Determines the output compression format based on command-line arguments or file extension.  
6. Allocates a context for encoding images.  
7. Selects the appropriate encoder based on the specified compression format.  
8. Sets encoding options such as quality, lossless mode, and chroma downsampling.  
9. Synthesizes an output filename if not provided.  
10. Encodes images or sequences based on the provided arguments.  
11. Writes the encoded HEIF file to the specified output filename.  
12. Cleans up and frees allocated resources before exiting.

full_code:
void show_help(const char* argv0)
{
  std::filesystem::path p(argv0);
  std::string filename = p.filename().string();

  std::stringstream sstr;
  sstr << " " << filename << "  libheif version: " << heif_get_version();

  std::string title = sstr.str();

  std::cerr << title << "\n"
            << std::string(title.length() + 1, '-') << "\n"
            << "Usage: " << filename << " [options] <input-image> ...\n"
            << "\n"
            << "When specifying multiple source images, they will all be saved into the same HEIF/AVIF file.\n"
            << "\n"
            << "Some encoders (x265, aom) let you pass-through any parameters by prefixing them with the encoder name.\n"
            << "For example, you may pass any x265 parameter by prefixing it with 'x265:'. For example, to set\n"
            << "the 'ctu' parameter, you will have to set 'x265:ctu' in libheif (e.g.: -p x265:ctu=64).\n"
            << "Note that when using the prefix, libheif cannot tell you which parameters and values are supported.\n"
            << "\n"
            << "Options:\n"
            << "  -h, --help                     show help\n"
            << "  -v, --version                  show version\n"
            << "  -o, --output                   output filename (optional)\n"
            << "  -q, --quality                  set output quality (0-100) for lossy compression\n"
            << "  -L, --lossless                 generate lossless output (-q has no effect). Image will be encoded as RGB (matrix_coefficients=0).\n"
            << "  -t, --thumb #                  generate thumbnail with maximum size # (default: off)\n"
            << "      --no-alpha                 do not save alpha channel\n"
            << "      --no-thumb-alpha           do not save alpha channel in thumbnail image\n"
            << "      --verbose                  enable logging output (more will increase logging level)\n"
            << "  -b, --bit-depth #              number of bits to use from an 16-bit PNG input, valid range: 9-16 (default: 10 bit)\n"
            << "      --premultiplied-alpha      input image has premultiplied alpha\n"
#if WITH_HEADER_COMPRESSION
            << "      --enable-metadata-compression ALGO  enable metadata item compression (experimental)\n"
            << "                                          Choose algorithm from {off"; // TODO: add 'auto', but it currently equals 'off'
  if (heif_metadata_compression_method_supported(heif_metadata_compression_deflate)) {
    std::cerr << ",deflate,zlib";
  }
  if (heif_metadata_compression_method_supported(heif_metadata_compression_brotli)) {
    std::cerr << ",brotli";
  }
  std::cerr << "}.\n"
#endif
            << "  -C, --chroma-downsampling ALGO force chroma downsampling algorithm (nn = nearest-neighbor / average / sharp-yuv)\n"
            << "                                 (sharp-yuv makes edges look sharper when using YUV420 with bilinear chroma upsampling)\n"
            << "      --benchmark                measure encoding time, PSNR, and output file size\n"
            << "      --pitm-description TEXT    set user description for primary image (experimental)\n"
#if HEIF_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES
            << "      --add-mime-item TYPE       add a mime item of the specified content type (experimental)\n"
            << "      --mime-item-file FILE      use the specified FILE as the data to put into the mime item (experimental)\n"
#endif
            << "\n"
            << "codecs:\n"
            << "  -A, --avif                     encode as AVIF (not needed if output filename with .avif suffix is provided)\n"
            << "      --hevc                     encode as HEVC (default)\n"
            << "      --vvc                      encode as VVC (experimental)\n"
            << "      --avc                      encode as AVC (experimental)\n"
            << "      --jpeg                     encode as JPEG\n"
            << "      --jpeg2000                 encode as JPEG 2000 (experimental)\n"
            << "      --htj2k                    encode as High Throughput JPEG 2000 (experimental)\n"
#if WITH_UNCOMPRESSED_CODEC
            << "  -U, --uncompressed             encode as uncompressed image (according to ISO 23001-17) (EXPERIMENTAL)\n"
            << "      --unci-compression METHOD  choose one of these methods: none, deflate, zlib, brotli.\n"
#endif
            << "      --list-encoders            list all available encoders for all compression formats\n"
            << "  -e, --encoder ID               select encoder to use (the IDs can be listed with --list-encoders)\n"
            << "      --plugin-directory DIR     load all codec plugins in the directory\n"
            << "  -P, --params                   show all encoder parameters and exit, input file not required or used.\n"
            << "  -p NAME=VALUE                  set encoder parameter\n"
            << "\n"
            << "color profile:\n"
            << "      --color-profile NAME       use a color profile preset for the output. Valid values are:\n"
            << "                                    custom:     (default) use the provided matrix_coefficients, colour_primaries, transfer_characteristic\n"
            << "                                    auto:       automatically guess suitable values from the input image characteristics\n"
            << "                                    compatible: use a profile that is decoded correctly by most applications by avoiding incomplete implementations\n"
            << "                                    601:        use Rec.601 (SD), close to JPEG\n"
            << "                                    709:        use Rec.709 (HDTV), close to sRGB\n"
            << "                                    2020:       use Rec.2020 (UHDTV), transfer curve will be selected based on bits per pixel\n"
            << "      --matrix_coefficients     nclx profile: color conversion matrix coefficients, default=6 (see h.273)\n"
            << "      --colour_primaries        nclx profile: color primaries (see h.273)\n"
            << "      --transfer_characteristic nclx profile: transfer characteristics (see h.273)\n"
            << "      --full_range_flag         nclx profile: full range flag, default: 1\n"
            << "      --enable-two-colr-boxes   will write both an ICC and an nclx color profile if both are present\n"
            << "      --clli MaxCLL,MaxPALL     add 'content light level information' property to all encoded images\n"
            << "      --pasp h,v                set pixel aspect ratio property to all encoded images\n"
            << "\n"
            << "tiling:\n"
            << "      --cut-tiles #             cuts the input image into square tiles of the given width\n"
            << "  -T, --tiled-input             input is a set of tile images (only provide one filename with two tile position numbers).\n"
            << "                                For example, 'tile-01-05.jpg' would be a valid input filename.\n"
            << "                                You only have to provide the filename of one tile as input, heif-enc will scan the directory\n"
            << "                                for the other tiles and determine the range of tiles automatically.\n"
            << "      --tiled-image-width #     override image width of tiled image\n"
            << "      --tiled-image-height #    override image height of tiled image\n"
            << "      --tiled-input-x-y         usually, the first number in the input tile filename should be the y position.\n"
            << "                                With this option, this can be swapped so that the first number is x, the second number y.\n"
#if HEIF_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES || WITH_UNCOMPRESSED_CODEC
            << "      --tiling-method METHOD    choose one of these methods: grid"
#if HEIF_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES
               ", tili (experimental)"
#endif
#if WITH_UNCOMPRESSED_CODEC
               ", unci"
#endif
               ". The default is 'grid'.\n"
#endif
#if HEIF_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES
            << "      --add-pyramid-group       when several images are given, put them into a multi-resolution pyramid group. (experimental)\n"
#endif
            << "\n"
            << "sequences:\n"
            << "  -S, --sequence                 encode input images as sequence (input filenames with a number will pull in all files with this pattern).\n"
            << "  -V, --video                    encode as video instead of image sequence\n"
            << "      --timebase #               set clock ticks/second for sequence\n"
            << "      --duration #               set frame duration (default: 1)\n"
            << "      --fps #                    set timebase and duration based on fps\n"
            << "      --repetitions #            set how often the sequence should be played back (default=1), special value: 'infinite'\n"
            << "      --gop-structure GOP        frame types to use in GOP (intra-only, low-delay, unrestricted)\n"
            << "      --min-keyframe-distance #  minimum distance of keyframes in sequence (0 = undefined)\n"
            << "      --max-keyframe-distance #  maximum distance of keyframes in sequence (0 = undefined)\n"
            << "      --max-frames #             limit sequence length to maximum number of frames\n"
#if HEIF_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES
            << "      --vmt-metadata FILE        encode metadata track from VMT file (experimental)\n"
            << "      --binary-metadata-track    parses VMT data as hex values that are written as raw binary (experimental)\n"
            << "      --metadata-track-uri URI   uses the URI identifier for the metadata track (experimental)\n"
            << "      --set-gimi-track-id ID     set the GIMI track ID for the visual track (experimental)\n"
            << "      --sai-data-file FILE       use the specified FILE as input data for the video frames SAI data\n"
#endif
            ;
}
purpose: Function purpose: "The function displays a help message detailing the usage, options, and parameters for a command-line tool related to encoding images in HEIF/AVIF format."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Extracts the filename from the provided command-line argument.  
2. Constructs a help message string that includes usage instructions and available options for a command-line tool.  
3. Outputs the help message to the standard error stream.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function processes a HEIF/AVIF file, decoding its image sequence and displaying the images while optionally showing metadata and track properties."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Initializes the libheif library and manages its resources.  
2. Parses command-line options for various functionalities such as displaying help, version, and metadata options.  
3. Validates the input HEIF file and checks for an image sequence.  
4. Opens the HEIF file and reads its contents into a context object.  
5. Retrieves and displays track properties and metadata if requested.  
6. Initializes an SDL window for displaying images.  
7. Decodes and displays images from the HEIF file in a loop, handling frame timing and metadata display.  
8. Releases resources associated with images and tracks before exiting the program.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function initializes an SDL window and renderer for displaying YUV video frames with specified dimensions and chroma format."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Initializes the SDL video subsystem.  
2. Creates a window with a specified title and dimensions.  
3. Creates a renderer for the window.  
4. Determines the pixel format based on the specified chroma type.  
5. Creates a texture for rendering.  
6. Sets the dimensions of a rectangle for rendering.  
7. Marks the window as open.  
8. Returns an optional error message if any initialization step fails.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function reads a box from a bitstream range, parses its header, creates the appropriate box type based on the header information, and handles various error conditions related to the box size and nesting level."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Parse the header of a box from a bitstream range.  
2. Create and initialize a specific box type based on the parsed header type.  
3. Perform security checks on the box size and nesting level.  
4. Wait for the required number of bytes to be available in the bitstream.  
5. Create a child bitstream range for reading the box data.  
6. Parse the box data and handle any parsing errors.  
7. Return the result of the box parsing operation.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `do_inflate` decompresses zlib-compressed data from a vector of bytes and returns the decompressed output as a vector of bytes."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Checks if the input compressed data is empty and returns an error if it is.  
2. Initializes a zlib `z_stream` structure for decompression.  
3. Allocates an output buffer for decompressed data and resizes it as needed during decompression.  
4. Performs the decompression of the input data using zlib's `inflate` function.  
5. Handles various error conditions during initialization and decompression, returning appropriate error messages.  
6. Appends the decompressed data to the output vector.  
7. Cleans up the zlib stream after decompression is complete.  
8. Returns the decompressed output data as a vector of bytes.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function adds and encodes a full grid of image tiles into a HEIF file, creating a new image item that represents the grid."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Creates an `ImageGrid` and sets the number of tiles based on the specified rows and columns.  
2. Encodes individual image tiles and stores their IDs.  
3. Creates a new grid image item and appends the grid data to the file.  
4. Connects the encoded tiles to the grid image item.  
5. Adds an ISPE property to the grid item with the calculated image dimensions.  
6. Copies the PIXI property from the first tile to the grid item.  
7. Copies over extra properties from the first tile to the grid item.  
8. Returns the created `ImageItem_Grid`.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function adds an encoded image tile to a grid, managing properties and references for the image within a file structure."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Encodes an image tile using specified encoding options and an encoder.  
2. Hides the encoded image tile in the file's information box.  
3. Assigns the encoded image tile to a grid based on its coordinates.  
4. Sets a property for the encoded image tile (PIXI property).  
5. Copies additional properties from the first tile to the grid item if it is the first tile.  
6. Adds a color profile to the grid item based on the first tile's image.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function encodes an image into a bitstream format while generating and adding various metadata properties related to the image's characteristics."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Encode a given image into a compressed bitstream using a specified encoder and options.  
2. Generate and insert color profile boxes into the encoded image properties.  
3. Calculate and set the image size properties (ispe and clap) based on the encoded image dimensions or fallback to input dimensions.  
4. Add common metadata properties (such as PIXI) based on the image's colorspace and chroma format.  
5. Copy extra image data into the image item and generate additional property boxes for the encoded image.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function encodes image tiles from a source HEIF pixel image into a vector of bytes, handling different color spaces and chroma formats."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Encodes image tiles from a source image based on its color space.  
2. Handles YCbCr color space by copying Y, Cb, and Cr channels into a data vector.  
3. Handles RGB color space with various chroma formats, copying pixel data into a data vector while considering alpha channel options.  
4. Handles monochrome color space by copying the Y channel and optionally the alpha channel into a data vector.  
5. Returns an error if the color space or bit depth is unsupported.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function extends the size of image planes to accommodate new dimensions while initializing new memory and filling any newly allocated areas with a specified value."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Extends the size of image planes to accommodate new dimensions.  
2. Allocates new memory for image planes if the current memory is insufficient.  
3. Copies existing pixel data from the old image plane to the newly allocated plane.  
4. Fills newly allocated areas of the image plane with a specified value.  
5. Updates the dimensions of the image planes and the logical image size.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function generates and returns a vector of shared pointers to various property boxes based on the presence of specific image metadata."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Generate a list of property boxes based on certain conditions.  
2. Create and add a PASP box if a non-square pixel ratio exists.  
3. Add a CLLI box if it exists.  
4. Create and add an MDCV box if it exists.  
5. Create and add an ITAI box if a TAI timestamp is available.  
6. Optionally generate and add NCLX and ICC color profile boxes based on the provided flag.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function checks whether the color profile associated with an image is defined as a non-undefined NCLX profile."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Checks if the color profile `m_color_profile_nclx` is defined.  
2. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the color profile is not undefined.  
3. Utilizes the `nclx_profile::undefined()` method to determine the state of the color profile.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `openh264_decode_next_image` serves as a wrapper that calls another function `openh264_decode_next_image2` to decode the next image using a specified decoder and security limits."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Defines a function `openh264_decode_next_image` that takes a decoder, an output image pointer, and security limits as parameters.  
2. Calls another function `openh264_decode_next_image2` with the same decoder and output image pointer, passing `nullptr` for an additional parameter.  
3. Returns the result of the `openh264_decode_next_image2` function call.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `openh264_decode_next_image2` decodes the next image from a H.264 encoded bitstream and outputs the resulting image in a specified format."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Check if there is enough input data for decoding and handle end-of-file conditions.  
2. Prepare and process the input data for decoding, including handling start code emulation prevention.  
3. Decode the input data using the OpenH264 decoder and manage the output buffer information.  
4. Create a HEIF image based on the decoded data and specified pixel format.  
5. Add image planes (Y, Cb, Cr) to the HEIF image and copy the decoded pixel data into these planes.  
6. Handle errors during the decoding and image creation process, including releasing resources if necessary.  
7. Return the status of the decoding operation and output image.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `openh264_push_data` serves as a wrapper that calls `openh264_push_data2` to push frame data to a decoder."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Defines a function named `openh264_push_data` that takes three parameters: a pointer to a decoder, a pointer to frame data, and the size of the frame.  
2. Calls another function `openh264_push_data2` with the provided parameters and an additional argument of `0`.  
3. Returns the result of the `openh264_push_data2` function call.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `openh264_push_data2` processes and stores a frame of data for an OpenH264 decoder, ensuring that the frame size is valid before adding it to the decoder's input queue."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Asserts that the frame size is greater than 4.  
2. Creates a packet structure to hold frame data and presentation timestamp (PTS).  
3. Inserts the frame data into the packet and adds it to the decoder's input data queue.  
4. Returns a success status indicating the operation was completed without errors.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function adds a chunk offset to a collection and sets a flag for 64-bit support if the offset exceeds a 32-bit limit."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Checks if the input size `s` is greater than `0xFFFFFFFF`.  
2. Sets the member variable `m_need_64bit` to `true` and calls `set_version(1)` if the condition is met.  
3. Adds the input size `s` to the `m_chunk_offset` vector.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `dump` generates a string representation of a `Box_container` object, including its own data and the data of its children, formatted with the provided indentation."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Creates a string output stream (`std::ostringstream`) to build a string representation.  
2. Calls the `dump` method of the `Box` class to append its string representation to the output stream.  
3. Appends the result of the `dump_children` method to the output stream and returns the final string.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function retrieves the chunk offset at a specified index from the `m_chunk_offset` vector, returning 0 if the index is out of bounds."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Checks if the provided index `idx` is out of bounds for the `m_chunk_offset` vector.  
2. Returns `0` if the index is out of bounds.  
3. Returns the value at the specified index `idx` from the `m_chunk_offset` vector if it is within bounds.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function calculates and returns the total number of samples by summing the sample counts from all entries in the `m_entries` collection."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Calculates the total number of samples from a collection of entries.  
2. Iterates through each entry in the `m_entries` container.  
3. Accumulates the `sample_count` from each entry into a total sum.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function retrieves the sample offset for a given sample index from a collection of entries, where each entry contains a sample count and its corresponding offset."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Iterates through a list of entries in `m_entries`.  
2. Checks if the provided `sample_idx` is less than the `sample_count` of the current entry.  
3. Returns the `sample_offset` of the entry if the condition is met.  
4. Decreases `sample_idx` by the `sample_count` of the current entry if the condition is not met.  
5. Returns 0 if no matching entry is found after iterating through all entries.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function `Box_container::parse` is designed to read and parse child elements from a given bitstream range while adhering to specified security limits."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Defines a function named `parse` within the `Box_container` class.  
2. Takes a reference to a `BitstreamRange` object and a pointer to `heif_security_limits` as parameters.  
3. Calls the `read_children` function with the provided parameters and returns its result.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function updates file pointers in a stream writer by adjusting each offset in the `m_offsets` vector with a given offset, while ensuring that the resulting values do not exceed the maximum limit for a 32-bit unsigned integer."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Store the current position of the writer.  
2. Set the writer's position to a specified offset (`m_offset_start_pos`).  
3. Iterate through a list of chunk offsets (`m_offsets`) and write updated offsets to the writer, handling potential overflow.  
4. Restore the writer's position to its original value.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function writes the metadata of a media box (mvhd) to a stream, including creation and modification times, timescale, duration, rate, volume, transformation matrix, and next track ID."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Reserves space for the box header in the writer.  
2. Writes metadata (creation time, modification time, timescale, duration) based on the version of the box.  
3. Writes additional properties such as rate, volume, and matrix values.  
4. Writes a fixed number of zero values for padding.  
5. Writes the next track ID.  
6. Prepends the box header to the writer.  
7. Returns an error status indicating success.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function initializes a SampleAuxInfoReader object by setting up sample offsets based on the provided SAIZ and SAIO boxes and the associated chunks."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Initializes the `SampleAuxInfoReader` with shared pointers to `Box_saiz`, `Box_saio`, and a vector of `Chunk` objects.  
2. Determines if there is only one chunk based on the number of chunks in `saio`.  
3. Retrieves the offset of the first chunk and the number of samples from `saiz`.  
4. Checks if the samples are contiguous and of constant size when there is only one chunk.  
5. Resizes the `m_sample_offsets` vector to accommodate the number of samples.  
6. Iterates through the samples to populate the `m_sample_offsets` vector with the appropriate offsets, adjusting for multiple chunks as necessary.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function retrieves sample information from a HEIF file by calculating the appropriate offset and size based on the sample index and returns the corresponding data as a vector of bytes."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Retrieve sample size based on whether the sample sizes are contiguous and constant.  
2. Calculate the offset for the sample data based on the sample index.  
3. Append data from a file range to a vector if the sample size is greater than zero.  
4. Return the vector containing the sample data or an error if applicable.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function loads a track from a given box, validating its structure and contents while initializing various properties and handling potential errors."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Load a track from a given `trak_box` and initialize various member variables.  
2. Validate the presence of required child boxes within the `trak_box`.  
3. Check the consistency of sample counts across different boxes.  
4. Create and store chunks based on sample descriptions and offsets.  
5. Read and validate auxiliary information boxes (`saiz` and `saio`).  
6. Read track properties from the `meta` box and extract content IDs.  
7. Perform security checks against defined limits.  
8. Initialize the sample timing table for the track.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function writes auxiliary information to a parent box in a HEIF file by appending child boxes and, if applicable, adding chunk offsets for data."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Appends a child box `m_saiz` to the `parent` box.  
2. Appends a child box `m_saio` to the `parent` box.  
3. If `m_data` is not empty, appends the data to the `file` and adds the chunk offset to `m_saio`.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function writes interleaved image data to a file and updates the chunk offset for the associated SAI (Sample Auxiliary Information) if the data is not empty."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Checks if the interleaved flag is set and if there is data to write.  
2. Appends the image data to a file and retrieves the position of the appended data.  
3. Adds the position of the appended data to a chunk offset in the `m_saio` object.  
4. Clears the `m_data` after writing it to the file.

purpose: Function purpose: "The function tests the creation and copying of a C++ object representing a color profile in an image processing context."
function_summary:
The functions of the code snippet are:  
1. Creates an image with specified dimensions and color space.  
2. Sets the NCLX color profile for the image using specified matrix coefficients.  
3. Retrieves the NCLX color profile from the image and creates a copy of it.
